questions.benchmark_description: Rearrange equations or formulas to isolate a quantity of interest.
questions.stem: In baseball, a pitcher's earned run average is used to measure how effective the pitcher is. The earned run average, A, is calculated using the formula A = 9E/N, where E is the number of earned runs and N is the number of innings the pitcher pitched. Which equation correctly solves the formula for the variable E?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a table, equation or written description of a linear function, graph that function, and determine and interpret its key features.
questions.image_note: Answer choices A-D are graphs. See source PDF page 3.
questions.stem: A function is shown: 7x + 4y = 14. Which graph represents the function? (Key points: A has (2,0) and (0,3.5); B has (0,2) and (3.5,0); C has (-2,0) and (0,3.5); D has (0,2) and (-3.5,0))

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, graph the solution set to a two-variable linear inequality.
questions.image_note: Answer choices A-D are graphs of inequality solution sets. See source PDF page 3.
questions.stem: An inequality is shown: 4x + 5y ≥ 10. Which graph represents the solution set of the inequality?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, write and solve one-variable linear inequalities, including compound inequalities. Represent solutions algebraically or graphically.
questions.image_note: Answer choices are number line graphs. See source PDF page 6.
questions.stem: A compound inequality is shown: 2x – 1 ≤ 3 or (1/2)x + 4 > 7. Which graph represents the solution set of the compound inequality?

choices.A: Number line graph A - see PDF
choices.B: Number line graph B - see PDF
choices.C: Number line graph C - see PDF
choices.D: Number line graph D - see PDF

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, write and solve one-variable linear inequalities, including compound inequalities. Represent solutions algebraically or graphically.
questions.stem: An inequality is shown: –3x + 7 ≥ –x – 5. What is the solution set for x?

questions.benchmark_description: Add, subtract and multiply polynomial expressions with rational number coefficients.
questions.stem: What is the sum of (2x³ + 4x) and (3x³ + 5x)?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, write and solve a system of two-variable linear equations algebraically or graphically.
questions.stem: A system of equations is shown: y = 2x + 2 and 2x + 5y = 19. What is the solution to the system?

questions.benchmark_description: Extend previous understanding of the Laws of Exponents to include rational exponents. Apply the Laws of Exponents to evaluate numerical expressions and generate equivalent numerical expressions involving rational exponents.
questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to ∛2?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a scatter plot with a line of fit and residuals, determine the strength and direction of the correlation. Interpret strength and direction within a real-world context.
questions.image_note: Scatter plot of U.S. national debt 2001-2016 shown in stem. Answer choices are text-based.
questions.stem: A scatter plot of the U.S. national debt from 2001 to 2016, including a linear equation and its residuals modeling the data, is shown. Which statement correctly interprets the scatter plot?

choices.A: The scatter plot shows a weak positive correlation.
choices.B: The scatter plot shows a weak negative correlation.
choices.C: The scatter plot shows a strong positive correlation.
choices.D: The scatter plot shows a strong negative correlation.

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, write and solve one-variable quadratic equations over the real number system.
questions.stem: An equation is shown: 3x² – 21 = 222. What is one exact solution to the equation?

questions.benchmark_description: Write an exponential function to represent a relationship between two quantities from a graph, a written description or a table of values within a mathematical or real-world context.
questions.image_note: Scatter plot of 5 points in first quadrant shown in stem. See source PDF page 13.
questions.stem: Five points from a function, f, are plotted in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane. The y-values range from 0.025 to 0.175 and x-values from 0.5 to 4. Which equation could represent the function?

questions.benchmark_description: Add, subtract and multiply polynomial expressions with rational number coefficients.
questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to (7k + 2)(5k – 9)?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a table, equation or written description of a linear function, graph that function, and determine and interpret its key features.
questions.image_note: Answer choices A-D are coordinate plane graphs. See source PDF page 15.
questions.stem: A function is shown: y + 1 = 2(x + 3). Which graph represents the function?

questions.benchmark_description: Determine whether a linear, quadratic or exponential function best models a given real-world situation.
questions.stem: Garrett buys a large bag of dog food. He feeds his dog 2 cups of dog food each day. Which statement describes the type of function that represents the amount of dog food in the bag after x days?

questions.benchmark_description: Given an equation or graph that defines a function, classify the function type. Given an input output table, determine a function type that could represent it.
questions.stem: Which table could represent a quadratic function? Table A: x=0,1,2,3 f(x)=3,9,27,81. Table B: x=0,1,2,3 g(x)=–2,–4,–6,–8. Table C: x=–1,0,1,2 h(x)=6,5,6,9. Table D: x=–3,–2,–1,0 j(x)=7,7,7,7.

questions.benchmark_description: Compare key features of linear functions each represented algebraically, graphically, in tables or written descriptions.
questions.stem: Which function has the greatest slope?

questions.benchmark_description: Graph the solution set of a system of two-variable linear inequalities.
questions.image_note: Answer choices A-D are coordinate plane graphs. See source PDF page 20.
questions.stem: A system of inequalities is shown: x < 3 and y ≥ –3. Which graph represents the solution set?

questions.benchmark_description: Solve and graph mathematical and real-world problems that are modeled with quadratic functions. Interpret key features and determine domain constraints in terms of the context.
questions.topic: Domain and Range of Quadratic Functions
questions.stem: A ball was dropped from a balcony at a height of 20 feet above level ground. The height, in feet, of the ball after t seconds is modeled by y = –16t² + 20. What is the range of the function in terms of the situation described?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, classify an exponential function as representing growth or decay.
questions.stem: Which equation models exponential growth?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, classify an exponential function as representing growth or decay.
questions.stem: Which function decays exponentially?

questions.benchmark_description: Write a linear two-variable equation to represent relationships between quantities from a graph, a written description or a table of values within a mathematical or real-world context.
questions.stem: A line with a slope of –0.2 passes through the point (11, 5). Select numbers to complete the equation of the line in point-slope form: y – [?] = [?](x – [?])

questions.benchmark_description: Divide a polynomial expression by a monomial expression with rational number coefficients.
questions.stem: An expression is given, where x ≠ 0: (4x² – 18x + 6) / (2x). Which expression is equivalent?

questions.benchmark_description: Given an equation or graph that defines a function, classify the function type. Given an input output table, determine a function type that could represent it.
questions.stem: An equation is given: y = (x – 3)² + 1. Which type of function does the equation represent?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a function represented in function notation, evaluate the function for an input in its domain.
questions.stem: A function is given: h(x) = (1/5)x². How can h(15) be evaluated?

choices.A: by multiplying 1/5 by the square of 15
choices.B: by multiplying the square of 1/5 by the square of 15
choices.C: by multiplying 1/5 by the square of 15, then dividing by 15
choices.D: by multiplying the square of 1/5 by the square of 15, then dividing by 15

questions.benchmark_description: Interpret data distributions represented in various ways. State whether the data is numerical or categorical, whether it is univariate or bivariate and interpret the different components and quantities in the display.
questions.image_note: Segmented bar chart showing travel method vs grade level. See source PDF page 29.
questions.stem: Maribel asked a random group of upper-class students at her school which grade they are in and how they traveled to school in the morning. The data are shown in a segmented bar chart with travel methods (Bus, Car, Walk) and 11th/12th graders. Which pair of terms describes the data?

questions.benchmark_description: Rewrite a polynomial expression as a product of polynomials over the real number system.
questions.stem: An expression is given: 42r³s² – 35r²s³. Which expression is equivalent?

questions.benchmark_description: Write a linear two-variable equation to represent relationships between quantities from a graph, a written description or a table of values within a mathematical or real-world context.
questions.image_note: Graph of a line shown in stem. See source PDF page 32.
questions.stem: The graph of a line is shown. Which equation represents the line?

questions.benchmark_description: Fit a linear function to bivariate numerical data that suggests a linear association and interpret the slope and y-intercept of the model. Use the model to solve real-world problems in terms of the context of the data.
questions.image_note: Scatter plot of U.S. population 1920-2020 shown. See source PDF page 34.
questions.stem: Darren determines the equation for the line of best fit to be y = 2.33x + 92.05, where y represents the population in millions and x represents years since 1920. Based on Darren's model, what is the projected U.S. population, in millions of people, for 2040?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a real-world context, represent constraints as systems of linear equations or inequalities. Interpret solutions to problems as viable or non-viable options.
questions.stem: Bashir has $3410 to invest in Account A (6% interest) and Account B (2% interest). He wants to earn at least $196 in interest. He puts x dollars in Account A and y dollars in Account B. Which system of constraints represents the situation?

questions.benchmark_description: Determine whether a linear, quadratic or exponential function best models a given real-world situation.
questions.stem: A rental company charges $2 per hour to rent a bicycle. A table shows: 1hr=$2, 2hr=$4, 3hr=$6. Which type of function models the price for renting one bicycle as a function of time?

questions.benchmark_description: Identify and interpret parts of an equation or expression that represent a quantity in terms of a mathematical or real-world context, including viewing one or more of their parts as a single entity.
questions.stem: An artist earns a profit from selling y paintings. His profit P is modeled by P = (200y + 23.25) – (10.53y). His profit is the difference in the amount earned from selling and the cost of making paintings. Which expression represents the cost of making y paintings?

questions.benchmark_description: Rearrange equations or formulas to isolate a quantity of interest.
questions.stem: Cardiac output D is found using D = V × R, where V is blood per heartbeat and R is heartbeats per minute. Complete the equation to represent V in terms of D and R. V = ?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, write and solve one-variable absolute value equations.
questions.stem: Two divers are underwater. One diver is at a depth of 32 meters. The equation |x – 32| = 11 can be used to find the possible depths x of the other diver. The other diver is at a depth of either ___ or ___ meters.

questions.benchmark_description: Compare key features of linear functions each represented algebraically, graphically, in tables or written descriptions.
questions.stem: The equation q(x) = 2x – 1 and a table for r(x): x=0,1,2,3 and r(x)=–2,4,10,16. Which statement correctly compares the slopes and y-intercepts of the two functions?

choices.A: Function r has a lesser y-intercept and a slope that is 2 times the slope of function q.
choices.B: Function r has a lesser y-intercept and a slope that is 3 times the slope of function q.
choices.C: Function r has a greater y-intercept and a slope that is 2 times the slope of function q.
choices.D: Function r has a greater y-intercept and a slope that is 3 times the slope of function q.

questions.benchmark_description: Graph the solution set of a system of two-variable linear inequalities.
questions.image_note: Answer choices A-D are coordinate plane graphs. See source PDF page 43.
questions.stem: A system of inequalities is given: x ≥ 5 and y < –3. Which graph represents the solution set?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, write and solve one-variable quadratic equations over the real number system.
questions.stem: An equation is shown: x² – 15x = –14. What are the two solutions to the equation?

questions.benchmark_description: Given a table, equation or written description of a quadratic function, graph that function, and determine and interpret its key features.
questions.stem: A function f is given: f(x) = x² + 12x + 20. Select terms to describe the end behavior. As x approaches –∞, f(x) approaches [A: –∞, B: ∞, C: 0]. As x approaches ∞, f(x) approaches [A: –∞, B: ∞, C: 0].

questions.benchmark_description: Apply previous understanding of operations with rational numbers to add, subtract, multiply and divide numerical radicals.
questions.stem: The expression ∛18 × ∛(6^(3/5)) is equivalent to x^(3/15). What is the value of x?

questions.benchmark_description: Construct a two-way frequency table summarizing bivariate categorical data. Interpret joint and marginal frequencies and determine possible associations in terms of a real-world context.
questions.stem: Margo surveys 150 high school juniors and seniors about favorite dipping sauces. Honey Mustard: Juniors=26, Seniors=22. Sweet and Sour: Juniors=11, Seniors=12. How many more students chose honey mustard than sweet and sour?

questions.benchmark_description: Compare key features of linear and nonlinear functions each represented algebraically, graphically, in tables or written descriptions.
questions.image_note: Graph of f(x) shown. Table for g(x): x=–2,–1,0,1,2 and g(x)=0.25,0.5,1,2,4. See source PDF page 48.
questions.stem: The graph of y = f(x) and a table of values for exponential function g(x) are shown. g(x) values: x=–2 → 0.25, x=–1 → 0.5, x=0 → 1, x=1 → 2, x=2 → 4. Which statement correctly compares the graphs?

choices.A: As the values of x increase, g(x) will eventually be greater than f(x).
choices.B: As the values of x increase, f(x) will eventually be greater than g(x).
choices.C: The value of the y-intercept of y = f(x) is greater than the value of the y-intercept of y = g(x).
choices.D: The value of the y-intercept of y = g(x) is greater than the value of the y-intercept of y = f(x).

questions.benchmark_description: Given a mathematical or real-world context, graph the solution set to a two-variable linear inequality.
questions.image_note: Answer choices A-D are graphs in first quadrant. See source PDF page 49.
questions.stem: Sanjay buys x boxes of hot wings and y bottles of soda. The numbers he can buy with $40 is represented by 40 ≥ 6x + 2y. Which graph represents all possible solutions for x and y?

questions.benchmark_description: Write two-variable linear inequalities to represent relationships between quantities from a graph or a written description within a mathematical or real-world context.
questions.stem: Janelle buys blackberries ($8/lb) and raspberries ($9/lb). She can spend a maximum of $40. Create an inequality to represent how many pounds of blackberries b and raspberries r she can buy.

questions.benchmark_description: Write an exponential function to represent a relationship between two quantities from a graph, a written description or a table of values within a mathematical or real-world context.
questions.stem: Cynthia's tools are worth $380 after 1 year and $361 after 2 years. Which equation represents function V?

questions.benchmark_description: Write a quadratic function to represent the relationship between two quantities from a graph, a written description or a table of values within a mathematical or real-world context.
questions.image_note: Graph of quadratic function shown. See source PDF page 52.
questions.stem: The graph of a quadratic function is shown. Which equation represents the function?

questions.benchmark_description: Apply previous understanding of operations with rational numbers to add, subtract, multiply and divide numerical radicals.
questions.stem: Select all the values that are equivalent to ∛(8/24).
